steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a completion that synthesizes both studies. Henry found investment increased in the three years after liberalization; Levine and Zervos found those gains did not endure over the full 1976–1993 period. The phrase "Taken together, these results suggest" requires a conclusion that captures both findings.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — consistent long-term gains: contradicts Levine and Zervos, who found effects did not endure. ✗
  • Choice B — economists' expectations: the passage makes no mention of what economists predicted. ✗
  • Choice C — benefits come later: reverses the timeline — Henry shows benefits come immediately, not after a delay. ✗
  • Choice D — an initial boost that fades over time: directly reconciles both studies — a short-term rise (Henry) that diminishes long-term (Levine and Zervos). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
